name.text: Entry Q1: What does "IT" stand for in a corporate context?

questiontext.text: What does "IT" stand for in a corporate context?

answer.text: Internet Technology
Information Technology
Internal Tracking
Integrated Tooling

name.text: Entry Q2: Which best describes a firewall?

questiontext.text: Which of the following best describes a firewall?

answer.text: A physical barrier installed in a server room
Software that speeds up internet connections
A network security device that monitors and controls traffic based on rules
A backup system for storing copies of important files

name.text: Entry Q3: What is a "log" in computing?

answer.text: A physical journal kept by IT administrators
A record of events or activities generated by a computer system
A type of file compression format
A backup of a database

name.text: Entry Q4: What does "IP address" stand for?

answer.text: Internet Protocol address
Internal Processing address
Input/Output Protocol address
Integrated Platform address

name.text: Entry Q5: Which is an example of a cybersecurity threat?

questiontext.text: Which of the following is an example of a cybersecurity threat?

answer.text: A server hardware failure
A natural disaster causing power outage
An attacker stealing user passwords through a phishing email
A software update that makes an application run faster
